---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis"
subtitle: ""
date: last-modified
sidebar: auto
number-sections: false
toc: true
author:
  - Jumbong Junior 

categories: []
tags: [""]
title-block-banner: false
format: 
  html: 
    mainfont: Times New Roman
    fontsize: 1.1em

jupyter: python3

---


```{python}
import kagglehub

# Download latest version
path = kagglehub.dataset_download("laotse/credit-risk-dataset")

print("Path to dataset files:", path)

```


```{python}
import os

os.listdir(path)
```


```{python}
import pandas as pd
file_path = os.path.join(path, "credit_risk_dataset.csv")
df = pd.read_csv(file_path)

df.head()
```


```{python}
df.dtypes
```

# Calcul du nombre d'observations de chaque modalité de la variable cb_person_cred_hist_length

```{python}
df["cb_person_cred_hist_length"].value_counts()

df["year"] = 2024 - df["cb_person_cred_hist_length"]
df["year"].value_counts()
```

```{python}
# The clip function ensures that any value below 2013 is replaced by 2013
df["year"] = df["year"].clip(lower=2013)
```


```{python}
df["year"].value_counts()
```


```{python}
obj_cols = df.select_dtypes(include="object").columns

df[obj_cols] = df[obj_cols].astype("category")

df.dtypes
```


```{python}
import pandas as pd

import importlib
import src.data_analysis_utils as utils

importlib.reload(utils)
from src.data_analysis_utils import create_quartile_bins, generate_categorical_report_excel, plot_series

eda_output_path = "/Users/juniorjumbong/Desktop/personal-website/00_tds/01_PD_credit_scoring/data_analysis_output/eda_output"

datasets_path = "/Users/juniorjumbong/Desktop/personal-website/00_tds/01_PD_credit_scoring/datasets"
```

# Distribution par anciennété ou par historique (cb_person_cred_hist_length).



```{python}

# renommer la variable loan_status def 

print(df["loan_status"].unique())

# [1 0] -> [True False
df["def"] = df["loan_status"].astype(int)

summary = generate_categorical_report_excel(
    df=df,
    category_col="cb_person_cred_hist_length",
    default_col="def",
    output_path=f"{eda_output_path}/cb_person_cred_hist_length_report.xlsx",
    sheet_name="Credit History Length",
    title="Distribution par anciennété ou par historique",
    category_label="Credit History Length",
    sort_by="count",
    ascending=False
)

print(summary)

```

# Distribution par année de début de l'historique de crédit (year).

```{python}
summary = generate_categorical_report_excel(
    df=df,
    category_col="year",
    default_col="def",
    output_path=f"{eda_output_path}/year_report.xlsx",
    sheet_name="Year",
    title="Distribution by Year of Credit History Start",
    category_label="Year of Credit History Start",
    sort_by="count",
    ascending=False
)
```

```{python}
# Distribution by age (person_age)
# Discretize the variable into quartiles

df_with_age_bins = create_quartile_bins(
    df,
    variable="person_age",
    new_var="age_quartile"
)

summary = generate_categorical_report_excel(
    df=df_with_age_bins,
    category_col="age_quartile",
    default_col="def",
    output_path=f"{eda_output_path}/age_quartile_report.xlsx",
    sheet_name="Age Quartiles",
    title="Distribution by Age (Quartiles)",
    category_label="Age Quartiles",
    sort_by="default_rate",
    ascending=False
)
```


## Application

```{python}
# Creation of the dataframe with the default rates by year and plot

rates_by_year = df.groupby("year")["def"].mean().reset_index()

plot_series(years=rates_by_year["year"], data={"Default rate": rates_by_year["def"] * 100})



```


# Creation de la base train$test qui est définie sur 2013 à 2021 et la base OOT qui est définie sur 2022.

```{python}
train_test_df = df[df["year"] <= 2021].copy()
oot_df = df[df["year"] == 2022].copy()  

# sauvegarde des bases
train_test_df.to_csv(f"{datasets_path}/train_test_data.csv", index=False)
oot_df.to_csv(f"{datasets_path}/oot_data.csv", index=False)
```


```{python}
import pandas as pd


```




```{python}
import pandas as pd


```


```{python}
bounds_table, train_clean_outlier, test_clean_outlier, oot_clean_outlier = apply_iqr_bounds(
    train_df,
    test_df,
    oot_df,
    variables
)
```


## COncernant la variable person_income, il je veux un code qui retourne le nombre d'observation inférieur à Lower Bound, le nombre d'observation supérieur à Upper Bound et le nombre d'observation entre les deux bornes, pour la base train.

```{python}
var = "person_income"
lower_bound = bounds_table.loc[bounds_table["Variable"] == var, "Lower Bound"].values[0]
upper_bound = bounds_table.loc[bounds_table["Variable"] == var, "Upper Bound"].values[0]
train_var = train_df[var]
below_lower = (train_var < lower_bound).sum()
above_upper = (train_var > upper_bound).sum()
between_bounds = ((train_var >= lower_bound) & (train_var <= upper_bound)).sum()
print(f"Variable: {var}")
print(f"Lower Bound: {lower_bound:.2f}")
print(f"Upper Bound: {upper_bound:.2f}")
print(f"Number of observations below lower bound: {below_lower}")
print(f"Number of observations above upper bound: {above_upper}")
print(f"Number of observations between bounds: {between_bounds}")
# min, max, mean, median de la variable person_income dans la base train
min_income = train_var.min()
max_income = train_var.max()
mean_income = train_var.mean()
median_income = train_var.median()
print(f"Min income: {min_income:.2f}")
print(f"Max income: {max_income:.2f}")
print(f"Mean income: {mean_income:.2f}")
print(f"Median income: {median_income:.2f}")
```






```{python}

```




## Importation des bases imputées pour vérification des missing values et des types de variables

```{python}
import pandas as pd
train_check = pd.read_csv("train_imputed.csv")
test_check = pd.read_csv("test_imputed.csv")
oot_check = pd.read_csv("oot_imputed.csv")

# Vérificaiton des values manquantes de chaque variable dans les bases imputées
print("Missing values in train_imputed:")
print(train_check.isnull().sum())
print("Missing values in test_imputed:")
print(test_check.isnull().sum())
print("Missing values in oot_imputed:")
print(oot_check.isnull().sum())

```
